mission.title: Install and Configure Kagent on Kubernetes
mission.description: kagent is a Kubernetes native framework for building AI agents, allowing you to easily deploy and manage AI workloads on Kubernetes. Installing kagent enables you to leverage its capabilities for developing intelligent applications in a cloud-native environment.

steps.title: Add the kagent Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official kagent Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kagent https://kagent-dev.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install kagent using Helm
steps.description:
Install kagent in a new namespace called 'kagent' with the following command:
```bash
helm install kagent kagent/kagent --namespace kagent --create-namespace --version v0.7.19
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the kagent pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kagent
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the kagent deployment to set resource limits. Create a YAML file named `kagent-resources.yaml` with the following content:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: kagent
  namespace: kagent
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: kagent
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
``` 
Then apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f kagent-resources.yaml
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
To access the kagent service, set up port forwarding with the following command:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/kagent --namespace kagent 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the kagent pods running in the 'kagent' namespace, and you will be able to access the service through port 8080. You should also see the resource limits applied to the kagent deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kagent https://kagent-dev.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install kagent kagent/kagent --namespace kagent --create-namespace --version v0.7.19

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: kagent
  namespace: kagent
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: kagent
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
